title: WitnessSolicitationRequest (v1)
description: Issuer relay's signed request to a federation peer asking it to co-witness a pending `append_only_horizon` retention cert. Peer verifies `issuer_signature` against `canonicalJson(cert_body)`, signs the same bytes, and returns a `WitnessSolicitationResponse`. See spec/relay-federation-v1.md §15.

kind.description: Discriminator pinning this body to the `append_only_horizon` cert arm. Witnesses for other deletion-cert kinds aren't part of phase 4b-3 — those arms use multi-signature semantics, not single-issuer + co-witnesses.

anyOf.description: Per-motebit horizon — issuer is the motebit identity key.
Operator-wide horizon — issuer is the operator key.

subject.description: Subject discriminator on `append_only_horizon` certs. Per decision 8, both per-motebit and operator-wide horizons are first-class.

store_id.description: Stable identifier for the audit log within the operator's deployment.

horizon_ts.description: Unix milliseconds. Entries with `timestamp < horizon_ts` will be unrecoverable once the cert is finalized. Witnesses attest they accept the horizon at this value.

issued_at.description: Unix milliseconds when the issuer signed the solicitation request. Same value lands on the eventual cert's `issued_at` field — load-bearing for the 24h `WitnessOmissionDispute` window clock.

algo.description: Closed registry of Merkle algorithm identifiers. `merkle-sha256-v1`: SHA-256 leaves, binary tree with odd-leaf promotion (no duplication). Mirrors the cert's `federation_graph_anchor.algo`.

merkle_root.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 root over the canonical peer-set leaves. `EMPTY_FEDERATION_GRAPH_ANCHOR` signals self-witnessed (leaf_count=0).

leaf_count.description: Number of peer pubkeys in the anchored set. `0` is the canonical self-witnessed encoding paired with the empty-tree merkle_root.

federation_graph_anchor.description: Mandatory from phase 4b-3 onward when the issuer has any federation peers at `horizon_ts` — `EMPTY_FEDERATION_GRAPH_ANCHOR` (leaf_count=0) signals self-witnessed deployments. Optional in the schema for grandfathered pre-4b-3 callers; verifier policy enforces presence-when-peered.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for phase 4b-3 federation co-witness artifacts: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature, hex public key. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

cert_body.description: Cert body the witness canonicalizes and signs. Drops `witnessed_by[]` and top-level `signature` from the cert — peer signatures are portable across witness compositions of the same body, and the issuer's final cert.signature is what binds the assembled `witnessed_by[]`.

issuer_id.description: Issuer's identifier — MUST match the id projected from `cert_body.subject` (motebit_id for per-motebit horizons, operator_id for operator-wide horizons). Disagreement is fail-closed at the peer.

issuer_signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature by the issuer's federation key over `canonicalJson(cert_body)` under `cert_body.suite`. Same canonical bytes the witness will sign — the request authenticates itself by the issuer's pre-commitment to the body, not by any per-request envelope signature.
